issues.comment: Dead code: Lines 115-116 each create a new ASN1Encoder instance, call write() on it, and then the returned encoder is simply discarded. The actual DER encoding is done correctly in the return statement on lines 118-122, which creates completely independent new encoder instances. Comparing with the Bouncy Castle reference implementation (BCECDSACryptoProvider.concatenatedRSToASN1DER), those two extra lines don't exist. These lines are clearly leftover from development/debugging. While the method does produce the correct output, this is dead code where encoder results are computed but thrown away.
